reasons: Kaja is one of the strongest direct answers because suppression can punish Martis even when normal crowd control is unreliable.
Martis loses a lot of fight value if he is locked before converting Mortal Coil pressure into Decimation resets.

proof.summary: Martis relies on control immunity during Mortal Coil, but suppression is an important exception, so Kaja can punish Martis during his commit window.
worksBestWhen: Kaja saves suppression for Martis instead of using it on the frontline.
Allies burst Martis before he can reset with Decimation.
failureCases: Kaja uses his lockdown too early.
Martis waits for Kaja's control to be spent before entering.

reasons: Franco gives another suppression-based answer to Martis's normal CC immunity.
He can punish Martis before Martis reaches the backline or after Martis commits too deep.

proof.summary: Martis can ignore many normal crowd-control effects during Mortal Coil, but Franco's suppression-style lockdown can still stop his fight flow.
worksBestWhen: Franco holds ultimate for Martis's real engage.
Franco's team is ready to burst during suppression.
failureCases: Franco misses hook and loses zoning pressure.
Martis forces Franco to use suppression on another target.

reasons: Karrie is a strong counter because her repeated Max HP-based true damage pressures Martis even when he uses damage reduction.
She works best when protected, because Martis can still kill her if he reaches melee range.

proof.summary: Martis uses damage reduction to survive close fights, while Karrie can pressure him with repeated true damage if she has enough space to keep attacking.
worksBestWhen: Karrie keeps distance while stacking damage.
Martis has already used Mortal Coil or cannot reach Karrie cleanly.
failureCases: Martis reaches Karrie before she can kite.
Martis executes a low-health ally first and uses the reset pressure to chase Karrie.

reasons: Alpha is a believable Martis counter because he can fight Martis directly with sustain while Beta adds true damage pressure.

Alpha is suggested because his sustain, close-range trading, and Beta's true-damage pressure let him contest Martis directly instead of depending only on crowd control that Martis can resist during Mortal Coil.

proof.summary: Martis wants to win close-range trades with damage reduction and control immunity, while Alpha can keep trading through sustain and Beta's true damage.
worksBestWhen: Alpha lands repeated skills to keep Beta pressure active.
Alpha has enough sustain or defensive item timing to survive Martis's first commit.
Alpha avoids becoming low enough for an easy Decimation reset.
failureCases: Martis bursts Alpha low before Alpha can sustain back.
Alpha misses skills and cannot trigger enough Beta pressure.
Martis enters when Alpha's ultimate or sustain window is unavailable.

reasons: X.Borg can pressure Martis with true damage while also having Firaga Armor as a survival buffer.
His ranged fire pressure makes Martis work harder to find a clean Decimation setup.

proof.summary: Martis wants to reduce damage and force close fights, while X.Borg can apply true damage windows and use Firaga Armor to survive part of the engage.
worksBestWhen: X.Borg keeps spacing and burns Martis before Martis reaches execute range.
X.Borg has Firaga Armor available before the fight starts.
The team prevents Martis from freely diving the armorless state.
failureCases: Martis catches X.Borg after Firaga Armor is broken.
X.Borg misses fire pressure and cannot trigger true damage reliably.
Martis reaches low-health targets and snowballs with Decimation resets.
